name: Macarthur Wind Farm
current_developer: Macarthur Wind Farm Pty Ltd and Meridian Wind Macarthur Pty Ltd
notable: Was largest wind farm in southern hemisphere at commissioning (2013). 140x Vestas V112-3.0MW. ~A$1B cost.

timeline.title: Victorian Government approval
timeline.detail: Planning permit granted by Victorian Government

timeline.title: Vestas turbine contract signed
timeline.detail: Deal for 140 Vestas V112-3.0 MW turbines cemented

timeline.title: Construction commenced
timeline.detail: Site clearance and civil works began Q4 2010

timeline.title: First turbine erected
timeline.detail: First of 140 turbines physically erected on site

timeline.title: Commercial operations commenced
timeline.detail: Commencement date from AEMO registration via OpenElectricity.

timeline.title: First generation recorded
timeline.detail: First market dispatch data recorded in AEMO systems.

eis_specs.document_title: Macarthur Wind Farm Environmental Effects Statement (VIC DSE EES)
eis_specs.hub_height_note: Standard 94 m hub for V112-3.0 MW on Western VIC plains (Macarthur site)
eis_specs.wind_speed_period: Mean annual wind speed at hub height; Macarthur, Western Victoria near Hamilton — from multi-year on-site met mast
eis_specs.energy_yield_method: WAsP modelling; P50 net yield after wake, availability and electrical losses
eis_specs.connection_substation_name: Macarthur 220/66 kV substation (new on-site), Western Victoria
eis_specs.connection_distance_note: ~5 km of 220 kV connection to AusNet Services SW Victoria network near Macarthur/Hamilton.
eis_specs.connection_augmentation: New 220 kV substation and line. Was the largest wind farm in the Southern Hemisphere at commissioning (2012). AusNet 220 kV required significant augmentation to handle 420 MW export.
eis_specs.notes: 420 MW (140 x 3.0 MW Vestas V112), near Macarthur, Western Victoria. Developer: AGL Energy / Meridian Energy (50:50 JV). EPC: Vestas. VIC EES approved 2009. Completed 2012. Was the largest wind farm in the Southern Hemisphere at commissioning. Groundbreaking project proving feasibility of large-scale onshore wind in Australia.
